commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Manual review is needed for the operator.read/list-time write contract and contributor-supplied live proof; there is no narrow automation-safe repair without that decision.; Needs attention: the diff makes a read-scoped proposal listing path persist canonical lifecycle status.; Review finding: [P2] Keep read-scoped listing from persisting lifecycle status — src/skills/workshop/service.ts:91 (sha=3a9f2f23512f9a091cd7e04063399b416d5ed605)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Skill Workshop listing to mark pending create/update proposals stale when their target skill already exists or is missing, extracts stale marking into a helper, and adds focused service tests.

PR surface: Source +49, Tests +68. Total +117 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main can leave a create proposal pending after its target `SKILL.md` appears, while apply only throws for the existing target. I did not run tests because this review was read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed for the operator.read/list-time write contract and contributor-supplied live proof; there is no narrow automation-safe repair without that decision.

target.title: fix(skills): reconcile stale workshop proposals when target skill exists or is missing
target.body:
## Summary

When a pending create proposal's target skill file already exists (e.g. user manually installed it after the workshop agent's apply timed out), or a pending update proposal's target skill file has been removed, the proposal stays pending indefinitely and the Skill Workshop UI shows a misleading waiting state.

## Fix

Add reconcileStaleProposals() called from listSkillProposals() to auto-mark such proposals as stale before the UI reads them:

- create proposal: if the target skill file already exists → mark stale
- update proposal: if the target skill file has been removed → mark stale

Also extract markProposalStale() as a shared helper, replacing duplicate inline stale-marking at three existing call sites.

## Linked Issue

Closes #90388

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Skill Workshop proposal can remain shown as waiting in the UI even after the skill has already been installed manually and is visible/active in the skills list (#90388). A pending create proposal stays pending forever when its target skill already exists; similarly for pending update proposals when the target is deleted.
- Real environment tested: Node v22.22.0, Vitest 4.1.7, Linux x86_64, temp workspace directories.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the workshop service test suite:
pnpm test -- src/skills/workshop/service.test.ts
- Evidence after fix:

Terminal capture:
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  24 passed (24)
   Start at  01:09:18
   Duration  6.69s (transform 1.00s, setup 388ms, import 1.32s, tests 4.72s, environment 0ms)

Three new reconciliation tests pass:
 reconciles pending create proposals as stale when the target skill already exists
 reconciles pending update proposals as stale when the target skill is missing
 keeps pending proposals as pending when no reconciliation is needed

Gateway proposals tests also pass:
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  10 passed (10)

- Observed result after fix: All 24 workshop service tests pass (3 new + 21 existing). All 10 gateway skill proposals tests pass. The reconciliation correctly detects pending create proposals whose target skill was manually created and marks them stale at list time.
- What was not tested: Live UI behavior in a running gateway. Update proposals where the target content changed but not deleted are already caught at apply-time by the currentContentHash check in readApplyTargetState(); reconciliation deliberately keeps these as pending.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
target.author: chengzhichao-xydt
